name: Keelung (Taiwan)
description: A yellow field charged at the centre with the Keelung city emblem.
history: Keelung is a provincial city on Taiwan's northern coast, occupying a deep natural harbour and serving as the principal northern port of the country. The current city flag has been in use since the late 20th century. The emblem stylises the characters 基隆 ('foundation prosperity') alongside maritime and harbour motifs.

features.symbolism: stylised form of the characters 基隆 with flowing maritime motifs, expressing the city's identity as Taiwan's principal northern port
